description: Create a documentation-only ADR for Hal's future team and organization factory control plane, defining the current local workflow boundary, future shared control-plane architecture, CLI contract compatibility expectations, operational concerns, and phased migration strategy without changing runtime behavior.

userStories.title: Create ADR Document Shell
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want the factory control plane decision recorded in the repository documentation so that future implementation work has a stable architectural reference.
acceptanceCriteria: An ADR-style document exists under docs/adr/ unless an existing ADR convention is identified and followed instead
The ADR title clearly references Hal's future team and organization factory control plane
The ADR links to ReScienceLab/hal#16
The ADR status is explicit as Proposed
The ADR states that the change is documentation-only
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Document Current Local Boundary
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the ADR to describe Hal's current local execution model so that future backend work does not accidentally break existing workflows.
acceptanceCriteria: The ADR describes local .hal/ runtime state as the current source of workflow state
The ADR describes per-worktree execution boundaries
The ADR describes local artifacts including PRDs, reports, logs, review outputs, and run output
The ADR states that current queue coordination is local rather than shared across users or organizations
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Document CLI Contract Boundary
userStories.description: As an agent integrator, I want the ADR to define which JSON contracts remain stable so that automation can safely depend on Hal CLI output.
acceptanceCriteria: The ADR covers hal status --json
The ADR covers hal doctor --json
The ADR covers hal continue --json
The ADR covers hal auto --json
The ADR covers review-loop and report artifacts where relevant
The ADR identifies stable contract areas
The ADR identifies additive extension points
The ADR states that backend implementation details remain hidden from CLI consumers unless explicitly added to a contract
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Define Control Plane Domain Model
userStories.description: As a future backend implementer, I want the ADR to define the core shared control plane entities so that future PRDs can build on consistent terminology.
acceptanceCriteria: The ADR defines users
The ADR defines owners
The ADR defines organizations
The ADR defines projects
The ADR defines project membership
The ADR distinguishes organization-level ownership from project-level membership
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Define RBAC Boundaries
userStories.description: As an organization administrator, I want the ADR to describe permission checks so that shared factory operations can be governed safely.
acceptanceCriteria: The ADR describes RBAC expectations for queue operations
The ADR describes RBAC expectations for run operations
The ADR describes RBAC expectations for artifact access
The ADR describes RBAC expectations for policy operations
The ADR identifies authorization as a control-plane responsibility
The ADR states that authorization internals are not CLI JSON contract details
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Define Shared Queue and Run Lifecycle
userStories.description: As a factory operator, I want the ADR to describe shared queue and run coordination so that multiple agents can cooperate without corrupting work state.
acceptanceCriteria: The ADR defines a queue item lifecycle from creation through completion or failure
The ADR describes run leases
The ADR describes heartbeat requirements
The ADR describes cancellation semantics
The ADR describes stale lease detection and recovery
The ADR describes concurrency expectations for queue and run transitions
The ADR describes idempotency expectations for queue and run transitions
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Define Shared Artifact and Audit Model
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want the ADR to describe shared artifacts and audit logging so that future systems are debuggable and accountable.
acceptanceCriteria: The ADR describes a shared artifact store for PRDs
The ADR describes artifact handling for reports
The ADR describes artifact handling for logs
The ADR describes artifact handling for review results
The ADR describes artifact handling for run output
The ADR describes audit logging for control-plane decisions
The ADR describes audit logging for state transitions
The ADR includes data ownership and access expectations
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Define Policy Inheritance and Overrides
userStories.description: As an organization owner, I want the ADR to define policy precedence so that organization, project, run, and local settings resolve predictably.
acceptanceCriteria: The ADR defines organization-level policy inheritance
The ADR defines project-level policy inheritance
The ADR defines run-level policy overrides
The ADR defines local override rules
The ADR provides explicit precedence ordering
The ADR identifies which local overrides should remain allowed for developer ergonomics
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Document Migration Strategy
userStories.description: As a future implementation planner, I want the ADR to outline a migration path from local state to a shared control plane so that future PRDs can be phased safely.
acceptanceCriteria: The ADR describes migration from local file state
The ADR describes migration from local queue coordination
The ADR preserves existing CLI behavior during early migration phases
The ADR includes a phased plan suitable for future PRDs
The ADR identifies compatibility gates before hosted or networked behavior is introduced
The ADR includes open questions for future implementation PRDs
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add Non-Goal Guardrails
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want the ADR to clearly state what is out of scope so that this documentation task does not become an implementation change.
acceptanceCriteria: The ADR states that no hosted service is implemented
The ADR states that no server, API handlers, database schema, auth integration, or networked queue are implemented
The ADR states that no runtime behavior changes are made to hal auto, hal status, hal doctor, hal continue, or review loops
The ADR states that no existing machine-readable JSON contracts are changed
The ADR states that no local auth, config, or state files are added for the future control plane
The ADR states that Hal-owned runtime state files are not manually modified
Typecheck passes
